title: PyroDash: Cost-Efficient Token-Level Small-Large Language Model Collaborative Inference
summary: PyroDash is a cost-aware framework for token-level SLM-LLM collaborative inference, reducing the costs of serving large language models at scale. It achieves this by dynamically allocating tokens between small and large models based on their complexity.
why_builders_care: Builders can use PyroDash to optimize the performance and cost of their language models, making them more efficient and scalable.

title: KlaatAI/klaatcode
summary: Klaatcode is an open-source AI coding agent for the terminal, providing Claude Code-grade accuracy with smart model routing. It supports multiple AI models, including Claude, GPT, and Gemini, and can cut costs by up to 10x.
why_builders_care: Builders can use Klaatcode to automate coding tasks, improve productivity, and reduce costs.

title: Look Less, Think Faster: Joint Token-Compute Adaptation for Multimodal LLMs
summary: This paper proposes a joint token-compute adaptation approach for multimodal LLMs, reducing inference costs by adapting both token and compute resources. This approach enables faster and more efficient processing of multimodal inputs.
why_builders_care: Builders can use this approach to optimize the performance of their multimodal models, making them more efficient and scalable.

title: How to Deploy Llama 3.3 70B with vLLM + Paged Attention on a $9/Month DigitalOcean GPU Droplet
summary: This tutorial provides a step-by-step guide on deploying Llama 3.3 70B with vLLM and paged attention on a $9/month DigitalOcean GPU droplet, achieving a 128K context window at a fraction of the cost of Claude Opus.
why_builders_care: Builders can use this tutorial to deploy and run large language models on a budget, making them more accessible and affordable.

title: GPT-Red: Unlocking Self-Improvement for Robustness
summary: GPT-Red is an automated red teaming system that uses self-play to improve AI safety, alignment, and prompt injection robustness. It enables the development of more robust and reliable language models.
why_builders_care: Builders can use GPT-Red to improve the safety and reliability of their language models, making them more trustworthy and effective.

title: Nativ: Run AI models locally on your Mac
summary: Nativ is a tool that allows users to run AI models locally on their Mac, providing a convenient and efficient way to work with AI models without relying on cloud services.
why_builders_care: Builders can use Nativ to run and test AI models locally, improving their development workflow and reducing dependencies on cloud services.

title: whisper.cpp
summary: Whisper.cpp is a C/C++ port of OpenAI's Whisper model, providing a lightweight and efficient way to run speech-to-text models on a variety of platforms.
why_builders_care: Builders can use Whisper.cpp to integrate speech-to-text capabilities into their applications, making them more accessible and user-friendly.

title: Livetennisapi/livetennisapi-mcp
summary: Livetennisapi-mcp is an MCP server that provides real-time tennis scores, odds, and model win-probability to AI agents, enabling them to make more informed decisions and take actions.
why_builders_care: Builders can use Livetennisapi-mcp to integrate real-time sports data into their AI applications, making them more engaging and interactive.

title: The Blessing of Dimensionality: How Near-Orthogonality in High-Dimensional Spaces Explains Temporal Portability
summary: This paper explores the concept of near-orthogonality in high-dimensional spaces and its relationship to temporal portability, providing new insights into the behavior of large language models.
why_builders_care: Builders can use this research to better understand the properties of high-dimensional spaces and develop more effective and efficient language models.

title: Notes to Self: Can LLMs Benefit from Experiential Abstractions?
summary: This paper investigates the potential benefits of experiential abstractions for large language models, exploring the idea of using abstractions to improve model performance and efficiency.
why_builders_care: Builders can use this research to develop more effective and efficient language models, leveraging the power of experiential abstractions to improve model performance.
